title: atriplex paludosa subsp. baudinii
intro: Atriplex paludosa subsp. baudinii, also known as the Baudin's saltbush, is a subspecies of the Atriplex paludosa plant, which is a type of shrub native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Atriplex paludosa subsp. baudinii include its ability to reduce swelling, treat skin conditions, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
